description: Synthetic deposition audio — Texas drawl 'Bright Spot' sounds like 'Brat Spot'

ambiguous_term.reason: 60-year-old Texas petroleum engineer. Long-i vowel collapses to short-a under drawl. 'Bright' -> 'Braht' -> steno hears 'Brat'.

lines.speaker: reporter
lines.text_spoken: We are on the record. The time is 10:00 a.m. This is the videotaped deposition of Earl Ray Thibodaux.

lines.speaker: reporter
lines.text_spoken: Mr. Thibodaux, do you solemnly swear that the testimony you are about to give will be the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth, so help you God?

lines.speaker: witness
lines.text_spoken: I do.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: Please state your name for the record.

lines.speaker: witness
lines.text_spoken: Earl Ray Thibodaux.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: Where are you currently employed?

lines.speaker: witness
lines.text_spoken: I am a senior petroleum engineer with Lone Star Reservoir Consulting out of Midland, Texas.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: How long have you been in the petroleum engineering field?

lines.speaker: witness
lines.text_spoken: Going on 35 years now. Started right out of Texas A and M back in 1991.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: Are you familiar with seismic interpretation methods used in reservoir analysis?

lines.speaker: witness
lines.text_spoken: Oh yes, sir. That has been the core of my work for the last 20 years. We use seismic amplitude analysis to identify hydrocarbon-bearing formations.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: Can you explain what you observed in the seismic data for the Yellowrock formation?

lines.speaker: witness_drawl
lines.text_spoken: Yes, sir. So when we ran the 3-D seismic survey over that area, we identified what we call a Brat Spot in the amplitude data. That high-amplitude anomaly right there told us there was gas-charged sand at that depth.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: What does that Brat Spot indicate specifically?

lines.speaker: witness_drawl
lines.text_spoken: Well, in seismic interpretation, a high-amplitude anomaly like that -- and that is industry standard terminology -- it tells us that the acoustic impedance contrast between the reservoir rock and the overlying shale is very strong. That is a direct hydrocarbon indicator.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: Are you saying that the Brat Spot is what led to the recommendation to drill at that location?

lines.speaker: witness_drawl
lines.text_spoken: That is correct. Without that anomaly in the seismic, we would not have had the confidence to recommend that well location.

lines.speaker: defense
lines.text_spoken: Objection. Leading.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: I'll rephrase.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.text_spoken: What was the basis for the well location recommendation in your report?

lines.speaker: witness_drawl
lines.text_spoken: The primary basis was the seismic amplitude anomaly -- this Brat Spot feature -- combined with the structural closure we mapped on the formation top.
